title: Puerto Banus Fishing Charter Half Day Guide 2026
html:
For a memorable morning on the Mediterranean, join a fishing charter half day in Puerto Banus departing from the marina at Club del Mar. Prices for shared charters start around 90 EUR per person and private trips from 600 EUR for up to six anglers. BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare options and rent a boat in Puerto Banus at the best rates.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

The main departure point is Puerto Banus marina itself, with easy access to the open Mediterranean. Nearby options include the fishing port of La Bajadilla in Marbella, and the chic marinas at Cabopino and Estepona. For a more secluded start, the small bay of San Pedro de Alcántara offers calm waters. Most charters head southwest towards Gibraltar or east to the waters off Fuengirola.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lists a variety of vessels suitable for a half-day fishing trip.

  1. Small motorboat (6, 8m) — ideal for a private group of four, from 350 EUR per half day.
  2. Centre-console fishing boat (7, 9m) — purpose-built for angling, capacity six, from 500 EUR.
  3. Cabin cruiser (10, 12m) — offers comfort and shade for six to eight guests, from 700 EUR.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (15, 26m) — such as the Maiora 26m, combines fishing with full amenities for up to ten guests; view this luxury yacht on BoatHire24 for details and pricing from 1,200 EUR per half day.
  5. Catamaran (12, 15m) — stable platform for groups of eight to twelve, from 900 EUR.

How much does a fishing charter half day in Puerto Banus cost?

Shared tour prices start at 80, 100 EUR per person, while a private charter typically costs between 500 and 1,500 EUR for half a day. The exact price depends on the boat size and included services.

  • Shared fishing trip: 80, 100 EUR per person, includes equipment and bait.
  • Small motorboat half-day private: from 350 EUR, rod hire extra.
  • Catamaran full-day: from 1,500 EUR, suitable for larger groups.
  • Crewed luxury yacht half-day: from 1,200 EUR, full service and tackle.

When is the best season for fishing in Puerto Banus?

The prime season runs from May to October, with July and August being the busiest months. The Levante wind can affect sea conditions, especially in July and August mornings, but afternoon trips often offer calmer waters. Spring and autumn provide excellent catches with fewer crowds.

Ask your charter operator to check the Levante forecast the day before; a light Levante can push baitfish closer to shore, improving your catch rate.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

If you take a crewed charter, no licence is required; the skipper holds all necessary permits. For bareboat rental, Spanish law requires a valid boating licence recognised in Spain. The operator will confirm the exact requirements for your nationality at booking.

At a glance

  • Price band: 80, 1,500 EUR per half day
  • Peak months: May to October
  • Trip length: half day (4 hours) or full day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

What fish can I catch on a half-day fishing charter?

Common catches include sea bass, bream, mackerel, and sometimes bluefish or small tuna near the rocky reefs.

Is fishing gear provided on the charter?

Most crewed charters provide rods, reels, bait, and tackle, but check with the operator before booking.

Can I keep the fish I catch?

Yes, you can keep your catch within legal size limits, and the crew often helps with cleaning or suggesting local restaurants that cook your fish.

How far offshore does the boat go?

Trips usually stay within 5 to 12 nautical miles of the coast, reaching depths of 30 to 50 metres for optimal fishing.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. For planning your trip or to become a host, browse the listings and discover verified boats for your next adventure in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
